untranslatable_reason: Pattern requires identification of: (1) steady upward climb, (2) bump/peak at resistance level, (3) rounding/rollover at peak, and (4) plunge back through earlier trendline. These are structural pattern features requiring multi-bar pattern recognition, trend line detection, and geometric shape analysis that cannot be expressed as simple indicator comparisons. The signal also lacks specific parameter values for trend identification thresholds and resistance level detection.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture core SHORT signal logic: high volume during uptrend (hvol_20 proxy for initial climbing phase) followed by close breaking below SMA_20 (proxy for plunge through earlier trendline). However, the BARR pattern is fundamentally a chart pattern requiring structural analysis beyond point-in-time indicator thresholds. Exit uses trend reversal (close crossing back above SMA_20). Average decline of 54% suggests setting hold_days_max conservatively at 20 days.
